game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Florence:** Florence is an interactive story experienced through a linear sequence of diverse, context-sensitive mini-games. Each vignette utilizes unique touch controls, such as dragging speech bubbles to form conversations, rotating puzzle pieces to complete tasks, or swiping to clean objects, directly reflecting the emotional beats and actions within the narrative. Successful completion of a mini-game, typically requiring 3-5 precise interactions or a short duration of 5-10 seconds, immediately advances the story to the next scene or dialogue segment. Progression is purely narrative-driven, with no traditional win/loss conditions or difficulty scaling; instead, the mini-games serve as interactive metaphors for Florence's emotional journey and relationship development. The core loop involves engaging with these simple, intuitive interactions to unfold the story, moving from one narrative moment to the next without explicit failure states, ensuring a continuous, emotionally resonant experience.
game_info.description: Experience Florence's emotional journey through interactive mini-games that tell her story. Each scene presents a unique interaction that mirrors the narrative moment - from the excitement of first conversations to the warmth of togetherness and the pain of heartbreak. Complete each mini-game by following intuitive prompts to advance through Florence's relationship story, from meeting someone special to self-discovery.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move/Navigate elements • Space: Select/Confirm action • Z: Alternative interaction<game_description>
Experience Florence's emotional journey through a series of interactive story vignettes. Each scene presents a unique mini-game that metaphorically represents a moment in her life - from forming connections through conversations, to building relationships through shared activities, to dealing with conflicts and changes. Complete simple, intuitive interactions to progress through the narrative and witness Florence's story unfold from beginning to end.
</game_description>

<game_controls>
Arrow Keys: Navigate selections, move objects, or interact with scene elements
Space: Confirm action, select, or interact with current element
Z: Alternative action or undo in certain scenes
Shift: Hold to modify actions or slow down movements
Enter: Start the game
ESC: Pause/Unpause during gameplay
R: Return to start screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic interaction mechanics across different vignette types. Validates that the player can successfully complete simple interactions like selecting speech bubbles, moving puzzle pieces, and performing basic actions. Ensures the game responds correctly to keyboard inputs and updates game state appropriately.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Perform random valid actions in each vignette type. For conversation scenes, randomly select speech bubbles. For puzzle scenes, attempt to move pieces. For cleaning/swiping scenes, perform directional movements. Continue interacting until the scene advances or a timeout occurs.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The game should progress through at least 3-4 vignettes without errors. The player should successfully complete interactions even with random inputs, demonstrating that the mechanics are functional and forgiving. The test succeeds if gameState updates correctly and no crashes occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the complete narrative progression from start to finish. Validates that all vignettes can be completed in sequence and that the win condition (reaching the end of Florence's story) is achievable. This represents an optimal playthrough that completes the entire emotional journey.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use intelligent, context-aware actions for each vignette type. Complete conversation scenes by selecting all speech bubbles in order. Solve puzzle scenes efficiently by placing pieces correctly. Execute cleaning actions systematically. Progress through all narrative chapters until reaching the finale.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test should successfully complete all story vignettes and reach GAME_OVER_WIN state. All chapters should be experienced in sequence without errors. The final score should reflect completion of the full narrative journey. Test succeeds when gamePhase becomes "GAME_OVER_WIN".

TEST_3.test_description: Tests edge cases and boundary conditions in vignette interactions. Validates that the game handles rapid inputs, attempting invalid actions, and boundary positions gracefully. Ensures the game remains stable under stressed input conditions.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Perform rapid, varied inputs including holding multiple keys, attempting actions at screen boundaries, and rapidly switching between different interaction types. Test each vignette's tolerance for unusual input patterns while still attempting to make progress.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game should remain stable and functional despite unusual inputs. No crashes or inconsistent state should occur. The player should still be able to make progress even with chaotic input patterns. Test succeeds if the game completes at least 2 vignettes without errors.
